gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.signInWithI.description: Signs in or signs up a user using credentials from an Identity Provider (IdP). This is done by manually providing an IdP credential, or by providing the authorization response obtained via the authori

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.verifyIosCl.description: Verifies an iOS client is a real iOS device. If the request is valid, a receipt will be sent in the response and a secret will be sent via Apple Push Notification Service. The client should send both

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.sendVerific.description: Sends a SMS verification code for phone number sign-in. To localize the text of the SMS sent to the user, set the HTTP header `X-Firebase-Locale` to the language code that corresponds with the user's

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.signInWithP.description: Completes a phone number authentication attempt. If a user already exists with the given phone number, an ID token is minted for that user. Otherwise, a new user is created and associated with the pho

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.signInWithG.description: Signs in or signs up a user with iOS Game Center credentials. If the sign-in succeeds, a new Identity Platform ID token and refresh token are issued for the authenticated user. The bundle ID is requir

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.signInWithC.description: Signs in or signs up a user by exchanging a custom Auth token. Upon a successful sign-in or sign-up, a new Identity Platform ID token and refresh token are issued for the user. An [API key](https://cl

gcp.identitytoolkit.accounts.sendOobCode.description: Sends an out-of-band confirmation code for an account. Requests from a authenticated request can optionally return a link including the OOB code instead of sending it.

gcp.identitytoolkit.v1.getRecaptchaParam.description: Gets parameters needed for generating a reCAPTCHA challenge.

gcp.identitytoolkit.v1.getProjects.description: Gets a project's public Identity Toolkit configuration. (Legacy) This method also supports authenticated calls from a developer to retrieve non-public configuration.

gcp.identitytoolkit.v1.getSessionCookieP.description: Retrieves the set of public keys of the session cookie JSON Web Token (JWT) signer that can be used to validate the session cookie created through createSessionCookie.

gcp.identitytoolkit.v1.getPublicKeys.description: Retrieves public keys of the legacy Identity Toolkit token signer to enable third parties to verify the legacy ID token. For now the X509 pem cert is the only format supported.
